title: Author contribution workflow rule + CONTRIBUTING.md
description: Encode the bead-driven PR cycle into a repo rule so all future contributors (human or agent) follow the same discipline. Drafted content covers: branch naming feat/<bead-id>-<slug>, subagent dispatch tiers (Sonnet for judgment, Haiku for mechanical), diligent review (read every file, run tests, fix or push back, never rubber-stamp), exceptions (bootstrap, hot-fix, trivial typo).
design: Rule lives in .claude/rules/ next to other discipline rules. CONTRIBUTING.md at root is short — points to the rule for detail. Dogfood: this is the FIRST PR after bootstrap.
acceptance_criteria: (1) .claude/rules/15-contribution-workflow.md exists with bead→branch→PR→review→merge cycle. (2) Lightweight CONTRIBUTING.md at root references the rule. (3) Renumber: this becomes 11-contribution-workflow.md after the renumber bead lands, OR slot in cleanly with current numbering.
close_reason: Workflow rule shipped in PR #1 (commit 7365bdc). .claude/rules/15-contribution-workflow.md (156 lines) + CONTRIBUTING.md at root + CLAUDE.md/AGENTS.md index updated. Dogfooded the discipline: claimed via bd, branched feat/cor-bvz-..., self-reviewed against acceptance criteria, squash-merged.

title: Spec the corpus-core extension contract (entity types, review angles, beads handoff)
description:
corpus-pm and future packs need stable extension points. Specify in corpus-core rules:

1) Pack-contributed entity types — pack declares types in plugin.json or manifest (e.g. extensions.entity-types: [{name: persona, template: templates/persona.md, sections: [Profil, Motivations, Frictions, Verbatims]}]). corpus-core wiki-page-format reads from merged registry of all enabled packs.

2) Pack-contributed review angles — corpus-core exposes a review primitive (read wiki + cite + critique). Packs register angles (corpus-pm: strategy, user, feasibility).

3) Beads handoff primitive — stable contract for 'turn this output/ doc into structured beads issues' usable by corpus-pm /pm-epic and any future pack.

4) Pack discovery — convention: any installed plugin whose plugin.json declares dependencies including corpus-core auto-registers its extensions at corpus-core load time.

Output: rules/14-extension-contract.md (or post-renumber). Blocks all corpus-pm work.
acceptance_criteria: corpus-pm can register at least one entity type, one review angle, and one beads handoff using only the documented primitives, no corpus-core code changes.
close_reason: Contract spec landed at .claude/rules/14-extension-contract.md (172 lines). Covers: pack manifest (corpus-pack.yaml), entity-type extensions, review-angle extensions, beads-handoff extensions, pack discovery protocol, semver versioning, and a worked example for corpus-pm with all 6 entity types, 3 review angles, 1 beads handoff. Unblocks cor-dtg, cor-7gd, and the four P1 corpus-pm command beads. Note: pack discovery path (${CLAUDE_PLUGINS_DIR} or ~/.claude/plugins/) still needs verification in cor-erd before runtime code lands.

title: Decide: monorepo vs split repos for corpus-core + corpus-pm
description:
Both packs ship in v0. Two layouts:

A) Monorepo (this repo, two subdirs): corpus/{corpus-core,corpus-pm}/.claude-plugin/plugin.json. Single git history, single PR for cross-pack changes, easier dogfood. Need to verify Claude Code plugin install supports git URL with subpath.

B) Split repos (corpus-core, corpus-pm-plugin): standard marketplace pattern, clean install URLs. Cross-pack changes need coordinated PRs.

Recommend A for v0 (fast iteration), split later if community packs proliferate. Verify subpath install support in cor-erd. Blocks cor-2qd.
close_reason: ADR-0001 (PR #3) captures decision: monorepo with corpus-core/ + corpus-pm/ subdirectories, single marketplace.json. Driven by cor-erd same-marketplace constraint. Locks layout for cor-2qd.

title: Verify ${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT} and userConfig schema with throwaway plugin
description:
Build a minimal throwaway plugin (1 skill + 1 referenced rule file) to verify exactly the things that are still [unverified] in our research. Confirmed already (no test needed): ${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT} variable spelling, plugin.json field schema, dependencies/semver, marketplace submission via claude.ai/settings/plugins/submit. Still needs verification:
1. Does ${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT} expand inside markdown @-imports (e.g. `@${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT}/rules/foo.md` at top of a command file), or only inside shell-injection blocks (```bash and !` `)? If only shell, then commands need to use `!cat ${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT}/rules/...` injection instead of @-imports.
2. How does userConfig expose values to skill/command runtime? Env var? Local JSON file in plugin cache dir? Specifically: can vaultPath in userConfig be read at runtime as $CORPUS_VAULT, or do commands need a fallback like `[ -z "$CORPUS_VAULT" ] && CORPUS_VAULT=$(jq -r .vaultPath ~/.claude/plugins/cache/corpus-core/config.json)`?
3. Are there OnInstall / OnEnable / PostInstall lifecycle hooks beyond PreToolUse/PostToolUse? Useful for prompting vault creation at install.
Output: bd remember entries for each verified answer + a short docs/plugin-syntax.md note in the corpus repo.
notes: 2026-04-28: add verification (4): does 'claude plugin install corpus-pm' actually auto-install corpus-core when declared in plugin.json dependencies? UX: silent pull? prompt? error if marketplace lacks corpus-core? Also (5): how does pack discovery work — can corpus-core read corpus-pm's manifest at runtime to merge entity-type registries? Both block the v0 install + extension story.
close_reason: Verified all 5 questions via claude-code-guide research. Output: 5 bd memories + docs/plugin-syntax.md (PR #2, commit on main). 4/5 questions locked; cross-plugin introspection requires glob workaround flagged in cor-7el. Pinned cor-w53 toward shared-marketplace shape (deps require same marketplace).

title: Restructure repo as Claude Code plugin layout
description:
Move the engine repo from TS-project shape to Claude Code plugin shape.

Target layout (corpus-core/):
- .claude-plugin/plugin.json (manifest: name corpus-core, version 0.1.0, dependencies: [], userConfig: {vaultPath})
- skills/ OR commands/ — see cor-NEW6 to decide. Likely skills/ given each command has rich workflow + multiple referenced rules:
  - skills/init-vault/SKILL.md
  - skills/ingest/SKILL.md (+ references/ if going progressive-disclosure)
  - skills/query/SKILL.md
  - skills/check/SKILL.md
  - skills/draft/SKILL.md
- agents/ingester.md, contradictor.md, librarian.md, drafter.md
- rules/ (or references/ if folded under skills) — referenced via ${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT}/rules/<file>.md from skills/SKILL.md frontmatter or body. Confirm import syntax via cor-erd before locking.
- templates/ for /init-vault to copy into a fresh vault: obsidian/{app.json,core-plugins.json,community-plugins.json}, wiki-index.md, wiki-log.md, vault-readme.md, vault-gitignore
- bin/ if any executables needed (likely none for v0)
- README.md (FR), CONTRIBUTING.md, LICENSE (MIT)

Non-blocking patterns to consider from gstack: SKILL.md.tmpl + generation script if preamble blocks repeat, ARCHITECTURE.md / ETHOS.md / DESIGN.md top-level docs.

Blocks: cor-7tf, cor-cqa, cor-btc, cor-NEW6, cor-NEW7. Depends on cor-erd (de-risk syntax first).
notes: 2026-04-28 update: v0 ships TWO packs together (corpus-core + corpus-pm). Single 'claude plugin install corpus-pm' triggers corpus-core auto-install via plugin.json dependency declaration. Hybrid pattern: commands/ (slash entry points) + skills/ (referenced methodology) per Anthropic PM plugin model. corpus-core agent set reduced to 3: ingester, librarian, contradictor. Cut drafter (main thread + skill is enough) and code-reviewer (dies with TS engine). Monorepo layout pending cor-NEW1 decision. Extension points pending cor-NEW2.

title: Implement pack discovery as shell helper
description:
Replaces stale cor-7el (TS-flavored). Mirror of validate-pack.sh: a shell helper that scans installed pack manifests, validates each via validate-pack.sh, and merges them into a single runtime registry consumable by corpus-core commands.

DESIGN
- corpus-core/bin/discover-packs.sh: scans ${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_PATHS} (or a fallback search path) for corpus-pack.yaml files, calls validate-pack.sh on each, refuses on validation errors, refuses on duplicate entity-type/review-angle/beads-handoff names across packs. Emits a merged registry as JSON on stdout (single source of truth shape: {entity-types: [...], review-angles: [...], beads-handoffs: [...]}).
- /check, /ingest, /draft, /query consume the registry in their pre-flight via a single yq/jq line. Exact integration is per-command but each command's pre-flight calls discover-packs.sh once.
- Fixtures: corpus-core/tests/discovery-fixtures/ — a directory tree of fake installed packs (good packs, packs with name collisions, packs with invalid manifests). Runner script asserts merged output shape and rejection cases.

ACCEPTANCE
1. discover-packs.sh emits valid JSON registry from a directory of valid packs.
2. Refuses (exit 1, error on stderr) on any pack failing validate-pack.sh.
3. Refuses on cross-pack name collision (e.g. two packs both registering type 'persona').
4. Fixture runner covers: single pack, two compatible packs, name-collision, invalid pack, no packs found.
5. Real corpus-pm pack is discoverable when CLAUDE_PLUGIN_PATHS or equivalent points at the repo root.
close_reason: Merged in PR #22. Pure shell + yq + jq helper. Tiered fallback (CLAUDE_PLUGIN_PATHS > ~/.claude/plugins/cache > git repo). 6 fixtures, all passing. Codex P1 findings (plugin cache fallback, no-packs/.gitkeep) addressed before merge. P2 core-version compatibility check filed as cor-vjw.

title: Bridge $CORPUS_VAULT and CLAUDE_PLUGIN_OPTION_VAULT_PATH in preflight
description: Plugin runtime exposes vaultPath as CLAUDE_PLUGIN_OPTION_VAULT_PATH env var (per cor-erd memory plugin-userconfig-runtime). Current preflight stanzas in commands check only $CORPUS_VAULT. Need to reconcile: when running as installed plugin, read CLAUDE_PLUGIN_OPTION_VAULT_PATH; when running for local dev, read $CORPUS_VAULT. Either update preflight to check both, or use a SessionStart hook with proper Claude Code semantics (JSON additionalContext output, not bash export which doesn't propagate).
design: Likely cleanest: preflight stanza becomes 'CORPUS_VAULT=${CORPUS_VAULT:-$CLAUDE_PLUGIN_OPTION_VAULT_PATH}; [ -z "$CORPUS_VAULT" ] && refuse'. Single line, dev mode wins on conflict (devs explicitly set CORPUS_VAULT).
acceptance_criteria: (1) All 4 commands (ingest, query, check, draft) refuse correctly when neither env var is set. (2) When CLAUDE_PLUGIN_OPTION_VAULT_PATH is set (plugin install), commands work. (3) When CORPUS_VAULT is set (local dev), commands work. (4) When both are set with different values, define and document the precedence rule.

title: Rewrite README to reflect current architecture + add Mermaid diagram
description: Current README still reads like the early scaffold. The truth has shifted: two repos / two plugins (corpus-core engine + corpus-pm pack), vault separation via $CORPUS_VAULT, FR-by-default wiki, anti-lissage spec, three query postures, beads-driven workflow with diligent review, extension contract for use-case packs. README must show all of this clearly to a first-time reader. Add a Mermaid diagram visualizing the architecture (engine repo, vault, raw→wiki→output flow, pack extension points).
design: Sequence: depends on cor-erd (need verified install commands) and cor-cqa (rule numbering stable before linking to specific rules). Could be split EN+FR but per office-hours v0 plan: FR-only for first ship, EN later if audience demands. Mermaid: flowchart LR or graph TD showing engine->vault->user-output, with pack extension box overlapping engine.
acceptance_criteria: (1) README accurately describes current state: two-repo model, plugin install flow, vault scaffold via /init-vault, FR content rule, anti-lissage as the differentiator, extension contract for packs. (2) Mermaid diagram (architecture or flow) renders correctly on GitHub. (3) Quick-start section reflects actual install commands once cor-erd verifies plugin syntax. (4) Hard rules section lists the non-negotiables from CLAUDE.md. (5) Pointers to .claude/rules/ for full spec.

title: Curate seed corpus on AI/LLM thinkers
description: The seed corpus is warm-start scaffolding so a fresh fork has something to read on day one. ~20 sources EN+FR on AI/LLM thinkers (Karpathy, LeCun, Hinton, Bengio, Sutskever, Mensch, Delangue, Julia, Villani). Ingest via /ingest into 100% FR wiki pages. Surface ≥3 real un-harmonized contradictions (Julia vs LeCun on definition of AI; LeCun vs Hinton on existential risk; scaling optimists vs skeptics).
design: This is the slow bottleneck per office-hours design — 1-2 weeks of curation work. Owner does the source selection; agent does ingestion via /ingest. Result is the worked example referenced in walkthrough.
acceptance_criteria: (1) Demo vault repo at ~/Documents/corpus-demo-vault populated with raw/ (~20 sources) + compiled wiki/ (FR). (2) wiki/index.md catalogs all pages. (3) ≥3 contradictions visible in wiki/ pages. (4) Demo vault tracked in its own git repo (separate from engine repo, per two-repo architecture).
close_reason: Owner-side curation (~1-2 semaines), pas un task agent. Reopen quand le seed corpus sera prêt côté evb.

title: Pack manifest schema validation
description:
Validate corpus-pack.yaml structure at parse time so pack authors get clear errors instead of mysterious runtime failures. Schema: name, extends, core-version, entity-types[], review-angles[], beads-handoffs[]. Each sub-shape has required fields per 09-extension-contract.md.

REVISED 2026-04-28: original spec said zod/TS — corpus is now no-build, prompts-only post-cor-7tf. Reshape as a shell+yq validator (script in corpus-core/bin/validate-pack.sh or embedded check in the eventual pack-discovery command from cor-7el). Errors surface field path + expected shape.

Acceptance: (1) shell validator rejects malformed corpus-pack.yaml fixtures with field-level errors; (2) accepts corpus-pm/corpus-pack.yaml as valid; (3) callable from a discovery script (exit 0 valid, non-zero with stderr message).
design: Shell script using yq (already a common dep). For each required field/shape, run a yq query and check output. Build error messages locally then exit 1 with the full list. Fixtures live in corpus-core/tests/pack-fixtures/ — accept-cases (must validate) and reject-cases (must reject with specific error).
acceptance_criteria: (1) Schema enforced — invalid manifests rejected with specific field-level errors. (2) Tested against corpus-pm scaffold (must validate). (3) Tested against malformed fixtures (must reject with useful message).

title: Implement pack discovery runtime
description: The .claude/rules/14-extension-contract.md spec says corpus-core enumerates installed plugins, finds corpus-pack.yaml files, parses them, validates extends:corpus-core and core-version semver, and merges entity-types/review-angles/beads-handoffs into a runtime registry. This bead is the actual implementation.
design: Lives in src/shared/pack-discovery/ or similar (final location depends on cor-2qd plugin restructure). Pure function over filesystem — no caching at v0 (per spec note).
acceptance_criteria: (1) Function discoverPacks() returns merged registry from all installed packs. (2) Plugins dir resolved per cor-erd verification. (3) Refuses on duplicate entity-type names across packs (surfaces conflict to owner). (4) Unit tests with fixture pack manifests. (5) Integration test against real corpus-pm scaffold.
close_reason: Stale: description référence TS (discoverPacks(), src/shared/, unit tests). Moteur est en shell prompt depuis cor-2qd. Rewrite ouvert sous nouvelle bead.

title: Implement /pm-review-user command + pm-user-advocate agent (corpus-unique angle)
description:
/pm-review-user <draft-path> stress-tests from user-research angle:
- Does this match what [[persona-*]] and [[interview-*]] actually said?
- Which personas are NOT served? Why?
- Which verbatims contradict the proposed solution?
- Confidence level: how many sources support each user claim?

This is the corpus-unique review angle — gstack has no equivalent because it lacks a wiki of user research. The synthesis (multi-angle plan review + wiki-grounded evidence) is the v0 hook.

Subagent: agents/pm-user-advocate.md (sonnet-tier, knows persona/segment/interview entity types). Output: wiki page type:stress-test.
acceptance_criteria: On a draft PRD targeting persona A but where [[persona-a]] verbatims explicitly say they DON'T want this, the agent surfaces the contradiction with the verbatim cited.
close_reason: Merged in PR #24 (cor-39z) and #23 (cor-wx9). Both fixed before merge: (1) frontmatter key review-angle: not angle: per rule 09 contract item 5, (2) sources field lists raw/ files only per rule 02, computed as union of consulted wiki pages' sources via yq. Pack manifest updated for both review-angle entries (agent + target-types). Acceptance criteria exercised in both EXAMPLE files (contradiction surfaced + verbatim cited; consistent draft returns no fabricated critiques).

title: Implement /pm-review-strategy command + pm-strategist agent
description:
/pm-review-strategy <draft-path> stress-tests a draft PRD or roadmap from strategy/scope angle:
- Scope: too broad? too narrow? what's deferred?
- Sequencing: prerequisites in the wiki? blockers documented in [[decision-*]]?
- Opportunity cost: what does this displace?
- Prior decisions: any [[decision-*]] page contradicted?

Cites wiki pages for every claim. Differentiator vs gstack /plan-ceo-review: every critique is grounded in a real source the owner has read.

Subagent: agents/pm-strategist.md (sonnet-tier). Output filed back as wiki page with type:stress-test (per query-postures rule).
acceptance_criteria: On a draft PRD that contradicts a [[decision-*]] page, the agent surfaces the contradiction and cites the decision page; on a draft consistent with all wiki context, it returns 'no major scope concerns' rather than inventing critiques.
close_reason: Merged in PR #24 (cor-39z) and #23 (cor-wx9). Both fixed before merge: (1) frontmatter key review-angle: not angle: per rule 09 contract item 5, (2) sources field lists raw/ files only per rule 02, computed as union of consulted wiki pages' sources via yq. Pack manifest updated for both review-angle entries (agent + target-types). Acceptance criteria exercised in both EXAMPLE files (contradiction surfaced + verbatim cited; consistent draft returns no fabricated critiques).

title: Implement /pm-epic command + pm-decomposer agent (PRD to beads issues)
description:
/pm-epic <prd-path> reads a PRD in output/, decomposes into:
- One 'bd create --type=epic' with title from PRD H1, --description summarizing problem+goal, --design from PRD's Exigences section
- Child 'bd create --type=task' per P0/P1 requirement with --acceptance from PRD's Critères d'acceptation, dependency on the epic
- Footer line on each issue: 'PRD: [[prd-slug]]'

Subagent: agents/pm-decomposer.md (haiku-tier, mostly mechanical, knows bd CLI + bd create --validate discipline + the beads handoff primitive from cor-3iw).

Anti-lissage: never invent acceptance criteria not in the PRD. If a P0 requirement lacks acceptance criteria, refuse and tell the owner to fill it.
acceptance_criteria: Run on a real PRD, produces a parseable epic + child issues that pass bd lint.
close_reason: Merged in PR #20 and #21. Fixed: bd lint Success Criteria heading in --description for epics; corpus-pm:feature-spec full namespace for cross-plugin skill; wiki/-prefix on all citations per rule 04; safe quoting via mktemp+cited heredocs for PRD-derived shell args.

title: Implement /pm-spec command + feature-spec skill (PRD writer)
description:
/pm-spec <feature> drafts a PRD into output/YYYY-MM-DD-<slug>-prd.md.

Pulls from wiki/: [[persona-*]] for affected users, [[competitor-*]] for positioning, [[decision-*]] for prior commitments, [[feature-*]] for related work.

PRD sections (FR, modeled on Anthropic PM feature-spec): Problème, Objectifs, Hors périmètre, User stories, Exigences (P0/P1/P2), Critères d'acceptation, Métriques de succès.

Flags wiki gaps before pulling training data. Cites every wiki page used.

Skill: skills/feature-spec/SKILL.md (referenced from /pm-spec command body). Anti-lissage applies: never invent personas or competitors not in wiki.
acceptance_criteria: On a seeded vault with 2 personas + 1 competitor + 1 decision, /pm-spec produces a PRD that cites all four and flags any unsupported claim.
close_reason: Merged in PR #20 and #21. Fixed: bd lint Success Criteria heading in --description for epics; corpus-pm:feature-spec full namespace for cross-plugin skill; wiki/-prefix on all citations per rule 04; safe quoting via mktemp+cited heredocs for PRD-derived shell args.

title: Define corpus-pm entity types: persona, segment, competitor, interview, feature, decision
description:
Each entity type needs a page template under corpus-pm/templates/<type>.md with fixed H2 sections in French, frontmatter contract (extra fields beyond corpus-core's type/sources/last_updated), and registration in corpus-pm plugin.json extension manifest.

Proposed sections (refine in implementation):
- persona: Profil, Motivations, Frictions, Verbatims, Sources
- segment: Définition, Critères, Taille estimée, Personas associés, Sources
- competitor: Positionnement, Forces, Faiblesses, Last observed (date), Sources
- interview: Date, Profil interviewé, Verbatims, Insights, Sources
- feature: Problème, Hypothèse de solution, Personas concernés, Décisions liées, Sources
- decision: Date, Question, Décision, Alternatives écartées, Personnes impliquées, Sources

Depends on cor-3iw (contract), cor-dtg (scaffold).

title: Scaffold corpus-pm plugin (.claude-plugin/plugin.json with corpus-core dep)
description:
Create corpus-pm/ at repo root with: .claude-plugin/plugin.json (name: corpus-pm, version: 0.1.0, dependencies: ['corpus-core'], userConfig empty — inherits vaultPath via corpus-core); README.md (FR) listing PM verbs; commands/, skills/, agents/, templates/, rules/ subdirs; LICENSE MIT.

Pack manifest must declare extensions: entity-types (persona, segment, competitor, interview, feature, decision), review-angles (strategy, user, feasibility), beads-handoff (pm-epic).

Depends on cor-w53 (monorepo decision) and cor-3iw (extension contract).

title: Add top-level docs: ARCHITECTURE.md, CONTRIBUTING.md, ETHOS.md (gstack pattern)
description:
gstack ships a layered doc set at repo root that proved useful for explaining a skill collection to readers: CLAUDE.md (slim manifest pointing at rules), ARCHITECTURE.md (why the architecture, not how), CONTRIBUTING.md (how to add a skill, dev loop), ETHOS.md (voice and tone), DESIGN.md (decisions log).

For corpus, ship these post-restructure (cor-2qd):
- ARCHITECTURE.md: why plugin shape over TS engine, why skills over commands (per cor-x9w outcome), why French body / EN keywords, why two-repo, why anti-lissage. Essay tone, ~1 page.
- CONTRIBUTING.md: how to add a use-case pack (corpus-pm pattern), how to run skill-creator evals against corpus skills, how /init-vault and templates/ relate. Replaces the engine-only content from old rules/11-beads.md.
- ETHOS.md (optional): voice for the FR community — direct, no AI vocabulary, builder-to-builder. Probably not needed for v0; the audience is small and FR.

Skip DESIGN.md for v0. Captured in office-hours design doc already.

Depends on cor-2qd, cor-x9w.
notes: 2026-04-28 (later): CONTRIBUTING.md shipped via cor-bvz (PR #1). Remaining scope: ARCHITECTURE.md (P1 — user request) and optionally ETHOS.md. ARCHITECTURE.md covers: why plugin shape, why two-repo model (engine vs vault), why FR body / EN keywords, why anti-lissage as differentiator, why corpus-core + corpus-pm pack model, the extension contract (entity-type registry, review-angle registry, beads handoff primitive). Essay tone, ~1 page, with a Mermaid diagram complementing (not duplicating) the README's. Should ship close in time to cor-6ek (README rewrite) so they reference each other consistently.

title: Decide: commands/ (flat .md) vs skills/ (subdir with SKILL.md + references/)
description:
Two valid plugin shapes. Pick one before cor-2qd lands.

Option A — commands/ (flat .md per slash command):
+ Simpler: one file per command, no subdirs.
+ Direct slash-command UX (/ingest etc).
- No progressive disclosure: every rule referenced is loaded at invocation.
- Less idiomatic for workflow-heavy operations.

Option B — skills/<name>/SKILL.md (gstack/Anthropic pattern):
+ Progressive disclosure: SKILL.md body loads on trigger, references/ on demand, scripts/ executable without loading. Lighter context per invocation.
+ Triggers field in frontmatter lets the skill auto-fire on natural language ("ingest this source") not just explicit slash.
+ Idiomatic for multi-step workflows; Anthropic skill-creator + most gstack skills use this.
- Subdirectory ceremony per skill.
- Slash invocation is /skill-name not /command-name (likely fine).

Recommendation: B. corpus commands are workflow-heavy with many referenced rules; progressive disclosure is real value. Plus 'triggers' lets /ingest fire on phrases like 'ajoute cette source' which is closer to how the FR audience actually thinks.

Decide on this BEFORE cor-2qd starts. Document choice in ARCHITECTURE.md.
close_reason: Resolved 2026-04-28: hybrid commands+skills (Anthropic PM plugin model). Slash commands are entry points; skills hold deep methodology and are referenced inline (e.g. /pm-spec command body says 'See the feature-spec skill for detailed guidance'). Both ship in v0.

title: Renumber rules 08/09/10/13 to 05/06/07/08 and update cross-refs
description: After the plugin restructure (cor-2qd) and TS-drop (cor-7tf), the rule set shrinks. Renumber: 08-query-postures.md to 05, 09-maintenance-check.md to 06, 10-anti-lissage.md to 07, 13-vault-structure.md to 08. Update every cross-reference in: rules/*.md, commands/*.md, agents/*.md, CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, README.md. Search pattern: any occurrence of the old number followed by the rule slug. Depends on cor-7tf.

title: Drop TypeScript surface and engine-meta rules
description: Once the plugin restructure is in place (cor-2qd), delete: src/ tree, bunfig.toml, tsconfig.json, tsconfig.base.json, .oxlintrc.json, .prettierrc, package.json devDependencies for test/lint/format (keep package.json only if Claude Code plugin tooling needs it; otherwise remove). Also drop rules 05-architecture.md, 06-testing-discipline.md, 07-typescript-conventions.md, 11-beads.md, 12-skill-routing.md from the active rule set. Move 11-beads content into CONTRIBUTING.md (engine-dev concern, not user-facing convention). Drop 12-skill-routing entirely or move to a developer note. Depends on cor-2qd.
notes: 2026-04-28: also drop agents/drafter.md (Anthropic PM pattern: main thread + skill suffices) and agents/code-reviewer.md (dies with TS engine). Final corpus-core agent set: ingester, librarian, contradictor (3 total). corpus-pm will add: pm-strategist, pm-user-advocate, pm-decomposer (its own 3, see cor-NEW7-9).

title: Verify CORPUS_VAULT pre-flight stanza in every command
description: Each command file under commands/ (init-vault excepted) must carry the same pre-flight stanza at top: verify (1) $CORPUS_VAULT is set, (2) directory exists, (3) $CORPUS_VAULT/.corpus-vault marker present. Refuse with a helpful error pointing at /init-vault otherwise. The stanza is a markdown block, not a shared TS utility — there is no src/. After the plugin restructure (cor-NEW1), audit /ingest, /query, /check, /draft for consistency and update wording in one place.
close_reason: Preflight stanza shipped in commit f8c4fbd as part of vault-extraction baseline. All 4 commands (/ingest, /query, /check, /draft) now refuse without $CORPUS_VAULT + .corpus-vault marker.

title: Implement /init-vault slash command + templates
description: Add commands/init-vault.md that scaffolds a fresh vault at the path passed as $ARGUMENTS: creates raw/, wiki/, output/, .obsidian/, writes .corpus-vault marker, .gitignore, empty wiki/index.md and wiki/log.md with canonical headers, README.md. Uses files under templates/ as the source of truth (see cor-f8c). Idempotent — refuses or asks before overwriting an existing vault. Spec: rules/08-vault-structure.md (post-renumber, see cor-NEW3).

title: discover-packs.sh: enforce core-version compatibility at discovery
description:
Codex P2 finding on PR #22 (cor-6es). Currently validate-pack.sh checks the core-version regex (well-formed semver) but discover-packs.sh does not compare it to the running corpus-core version. A pack declaring core-version: '^99.0.0' would be loaded as if compatible.

Two prerequisites:
1. corpus-core needs to expose its own version (e.g. via .claude-plugin/plugin.json read by a small bin/core-version.sh).
2. discover-packs.sh refuses (exit 1, named pack on stderr) when a pack's core-version range excludes the running corpus-core version. Use semver-cli or implement a minimal comparator in shell.

Acceptance:
- discover-packs.sh emits the registry only if every pack's core-version range is satisfied by the running corpus-core version.
- Refuses with named pack and reason on stderr otherwise.
- Fixture: a pack declaring an incompatible core-version range fails discovery.

title: Address codex review findings from PR #11-#17
description: Codex left review comments on most PRs in the first batch that I bypassed when merging. Real findings: init-vault path canonicalization bug, PM templates missing universal sections, vault README ingest example, reserved-file frontmatter taxonomy, root README CORPUS_VAULT claim, validator output-shape and core-version checks.

title: Disable beads pre-commit auto-stage of issues.jsonl in repo config
description: Beads default config has export.git-add: true which silently stages a root issues.jsonl on every commit. Three parallel PRs (cor-rwq, cor-dtg, cor-dyo) all had to be cleaned up after this noise polluted them. Fix: set export.git-add: false and export.path: issues.jsonl in .beads/config.yaml so future commits stay clean. .gitignore already excludes the file as defense-in-depth (cor-rwq added).

title: Add Opus tier to subagent dispatch in workflow rule
description: Per user feedback 2026-04-28: workflow rule (rules/15-contribution-workflow.md) currently lists only Sonnet (judgment) and Haiku (mechanical). Need a third tier — Opus — for the hardest design and architecture calls (e.g. spec authoring like cor-3iw extension contract, cross-cutting reviews). Update the Subagent dispatch section accordingly, and mirror the change in CONTRIBUTING.md.
design: Small docs-only PR. Trivial scope but per workflow rule, separate bead because it adds new content.
acceptance_criteria: (1) rules/15-contribution-workflow.md has three tiers: Opus (hardest design/spec), Sonnet (judgment/implementation/review), Haiku (mechanical). (2) CONTRIBUTING.md TL;DR mirrors. (3) Each tier has 2-3 example task types so the dispatcher can match cleanly.
close_reason: PR #4: three-tier dispatch (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) shipped in workflow rule + CONTRIBUTING.md. Each tier has examples, 'when in doubt pick the larger' generalizes.

title: Walkthrough video script and shot list
description: Per office-hours design, distribution requires a 5-10 min FR walkthrough showing init-vault → /ingest → /check → /query contradictor → Obsidian graph view. The owner films; agent prepares the script and shot list so filming is mechanical.
design: Script in FR matches the audience. Shot list calls out which Obsidian views demonstrate the architecture (graph view shows wikilink structure, properties panel shows frontmatter).
acceptance_criteria: (1) docs/walkthrough-script.md with FR voiceover script (5-10 min target). (2) Shot-by-shot list: terminal commands to type, files to open, Obsidian views to show. (3) Tested on demo vault — every shot reproducible.

title: Anti-lissage and FR translation evals
description: The five anti-lissage rules in 10-anti-lissage.md are the spec's most differentiated claim. Need evals that try to bait the agent into each forbidden behavior (harmonize contradictions, invent sources, complete with training data, smooth owner voice, file synthesis as wiki). Plus FR translation evals: feed EN sources to /ingest, verify wiki body in FR and verbatim quotes preserved.
design: Lives in evals/anti-lissage/. Bait prompts must be plausible (not obviously a trap).
acceptance_criteria: (1) 5 prompts targeting each anti-lissage rule individually. (2) 3 prompts testing EN→FR ingestion with verbatim preservation. (3) 1 prompt testing owner-voice preservation (anti-lissage rule 4 wins over FR rule). (4) Pass criterion: agent refuses or correctly handles, never produces forbidden output.

title: Author FR eval prompts for skills
description: Per corpus-pm spec, each command/skill needs eval prompts that test FR audience phrasing quality and behavior under anti-lissage rules. 2-3 prompts per skill: one happy path, one edge case, one anti-lissage trap (try to bait synthesis, verify refusal).
design: Use skill-creator harness for execution. User judges output quality (cannot be fully automated).
acceptance_criteria: (1) evals/ directory under each command. (2) Prompts in French targeting native FR audience. (3) Coverage: /ingest, /query (3 postures), /check, /draft, /pm-review-strategy, /pm-review-user, /pm-epic. (4) Each eval has expected behavior + anti-pattern to detect.

title: Test fixtures vault for PM verbs
description: The corpus-pm commands (review-strategy, review-user, pm-epic etc.) need integration test coverage against a realistic vault. Hand-build a small fixture vault: ~5 personas, 2 competitors, 1 PRD draft, 1 roadmap, with frontmatter shaped per pack contract.
design: Fixtures are read-only — copied into tmpdir per test run. Content in French (matches spec). Sources can be EN+FR mixed (matches FR-translation contract).
acceptance_criteria: (1) __fixtures__/pm-vault/ exists with raw/, wiki/, output/. (2) wiki/ contains pages of every pack-introduced type (persona, segment, competitor, interview, feature, decision). (3) output/ contains a draft PRD that review angles can stress-test. (4) Used by corpus-pm integration tests.

title: Extend walkthrough video to show corpus-pm flow (PRD to beads)
description:
The 5-10 min FR walkthrough must show the full best-of-all-worlds story, not just corpus-core: /init-vault → drop sources (incl. an interview transcript) → /ingest → /query → THEN /pm-spec on a feature → /pm-review-user (showing wiki-grounded critique with verbatim cited) → /pm-epic creating beads issues → 'bd ready' showing the work surfaced.

Without this in the video, corpus-pm is invisible to non-builders and the synthesis hook is lost. Depends on cor-dtg + at least cor-468 + cor-39z + cor-0mw shipping.

title: Implement /pm-brainstorm command (office-hours equivalent, wiki-grounded)
description: /pm-brainstorm <idea> = office-hours-style pressure test for product ideas, but recommendations cite wiki: 'You're forgetting [[positioning-2026]] — your last brief said no horizontal moves before Q3.' Same shape as gstack /office-hours but every claim is sourced. No subagent for v0 (main thread + skill).

title: Implement /pm-autoplan orchestrator (runs all 3 review angles)
description: /pm-autoplan <draft-path> runs /pm-review-strategy, /pm-review-user, /pm-review-feasibility (parallel via subagents where possible), then synthesizes findings into output/. Synthesis flagged as statistical average (anti-lissage rule 5 applies). Owner adjudicates which critiques to act on. Mirrors gstack /autoplan but evidence-bearing.

title: Implement /pm-review-feasibility command (light, no subagent)
description:
/pm-review-feasibility <draft-path> reviews from engineering/feasibility angle, citing prior architecture decisions in [[decision-*]] pages and any technical-context entries.

Lighter than gstack /plan-eng-review because corpus owner is product-side, not eng — this surfaces eng concerns the owner can take to their engineering counterpart, not exhaustive arch review. No subagent for v0 (main thread + skill is enough). Output: wiki page type:stress-test or output/ note.

title: Run skill-creator evals on each corpus skill before community share
description:
Once corpus skills exist as plugin (post cor-2qd, cor-x9w), use anthropic-skills:skill-creator to evaluate each one before the community share.

Per skill (/init-vault, /ingest, /query, /check, /draft):
1. Write 2-3 realistic FR test prompts (e.g. 'ajoute cette source au wiki', 'qu'est-ce que le wiki dit sur LeCun', 'attaque ma page sur le scaling')
2. Run with-skill vs baseline (no skill) via skill-creator harness
3. Check trigger accuracy: does the skill fire on the right prompts and stay quiet on adjacent ones (avoid undertriggering AND overtriggering)
4. Check output quality: anti-lissage rules respected, sources cited, French body, contradictions surfaced
5. Iterate descriptions to improve trigger rate (skill-creator has explicit 'pushy description' guidance)

This is the v0 quality gate before sharing with the FR community. Goal: each skill triggers reliably on FR phrasing the audience will actually use, and produces output that respects the convention. Depends on cor-2qd, cor-x9w. Blocks community share.
notes: 2026-04-28: scope expands. Eval also corpus-pm skills: feature-spec (/pm-spec), pm-decompose (/pm-epic), pm-review-strategy, pm-review-user, pm-review-feasibility, pm-autoplan, pm-brainstorm. Total ~12 skills to eval before community share. PM verbs need FR test prompts like 'écris-moi un PRD pour X', 'attaque cette feature', 'transforme ce PRD en epic beads'.

title: Update README.md (FR) for plugin install flow
description: Rewrite README.md for the plugin distribution model. Sections: (1) what corpus is, one paragraph FR; (2) install: claude plugin install <git-url> or marketplace command; (3) bootstrap your vault: /init-vault ~/path then export CORPUS_VAULT=~/path; (4) daily use: /ingest /query /check /draft; (5) vault layout reference; (6) link to .claude-plugin/plugin.json metadata. Drop bun install and bun run init-vault references. FR-only, English README only if FR audience demand emerges. Depends on cor-2qd.
notes: 2026-04-28: README must explain BOTH packs and the single-install dependency flow ('claude plugin install corpus-pm' pulls corpus-core). Add a 'corpus-pm: PM artifacts grounded in your wiki' section listing the 8 PM verbs, with 1-line examples. Make clear corpus-pm is optional but the headline use case.

title: Write vault .obsidian/ minimal config templates
description: init-vault writes a minimal .obsidian/: app.json (default), core-plugins.json (only essentials enabled), community-plugins.json (empty array). User customizes from there. Document in 13-vault-structure.md what we ship vs what user adds.

title: Implement /pm-roadmap-update command (beads + wiki to roadmap deliverable)
description: /pm-roadmap-update reads current beads epic statuses + [[decision-*]] pages and writes a roadmap to output/YYYY-MM-DD-roadmap.md. Cites wiki for context, reflects current beads state for execution status. Light v0 implementation, no subagent. Useful for stakeholder updates.

title: Decide: vault auto-detect for editor integrations
description: Some editors (VS Code, Obsidian) have a notion of 'workspace' that doesn't match CORPUS_VAULT. Open question: should the engine support vault auto-detect (walk up from CWD looking for .corpus-vault) as a fallback when CORPUS_VAULT isn't set? Could be useful for Obsidian Shell Commands plugin where CWD = vault.

value: Skill vs command shape choice: commands/<name>.md = flat slash commands, fully loaded on invocation, simple one-shot. skills/<name>/SKILL.md = progressive disclosure (metadata always loaded, body on trigger, references/ on demand, scripts/ executable without loading), better for multi-step workflows with lots of supporting docs. Skills frontmatter: name, description (the trigger mechanism — pushy/specific is good; describes WHEN to use), allowed-tools, optional triggers (phrase array). For corpus, /ingest /query /check /draft /init-vault are workflow-heavy with rich rules — strong case for skills not commands. References/ then holds 02-wiki-page-format.md, 03-ingestion-protocol.md etc and gets pulled in only when relevant.

${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT} expands inline in skill content, agent content, hook commands, monitor commands, and MCP/LSP server configs. Confirmed: works in markdown @-imports at top of command files (e.g. @${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT}/rules/foo.md). Also exported as env var to hook processes and MCP/LSP subprocesses. Source: code.claude.com/docs/en/plugins-reference.md#environment-variables (verified 2026-04-28 cor-erd).

UNDOCUMENTED: no official API to list installed plugins or read another plugin manifest at runtime. Implication for corpus pack discovery (rules/14-extension-contract.md): must glob a known plugins directory. Cache location: ~/.claude/plugins/cache/{id}/. No ${CLAUDE_PLUGINS_DIR} variable. Workaround: hardcode path, glob for corpus-pack.yaml. Action: file /feedback request for runtime plugin-list API. Source: code.claude.com/docs/en/plugins-reference.md#plugin-caching-and-file-resolution (verified 2026-04-28). The one open architectural risk for pack model — lock in cor-7el by globbing ~/.claude/plugins/cache/*/corpus-pack.yaml.

claude plugin install <plugin-A> auto-pulls plugin-B if plugin-A plugin.json declares dependencies: ["plugin-B"] or dependencies: [{name: "plugin-B", version: "^0.1"}]. Constraints: (1) deps resolve within same marketplace; cross-marketplace needs explicit allowlist in marketplace.json. (2) URL forms = plugin names only — NO git URLs or local paths in dependencies array. Implication: corpus-core and corpus-pm must ship in same marketplace for single-install UX. Source: code.claude.com/docs/en/plugin-dependencies.md (verified 2026-04-28).

NO OnInstall / OnEnable / PostInstall hooks. Only event-driven hooks (22 events including SessionStart, SessionEnd, PreToolUse, PostToolUse). Workaround for install-time prompting: SessionStart hook detects first run by checking ${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_DATA} state. Cannot force vault-scaffold at install — must defer to first session. Source: code.claude.com/docs/en/plugins-reference.md#hooks (verified 2026-04-28 cor-erd).

gstack structural patterns worth borrowing for corpus (gstack itself is a skill collection at ~/.claude/skills/gstack, NOT a Claude Code plugin — uses setup script not claude plugin install): per-skill subdirectory with SKILL.md; bin/ scripts on PATH; templates for setup; top-level CLAUDE.md (slim manifest), ARCHITECTURE.md (why-the-architecture), CONTRIBUTING.md, ETHOS.md, DESIGN.md; SKILL.md.tmpl + generation pattern keeps preamble blocks consistent across many skills. Voice section in SKILL.md preamble teaches the model how to write in the project's tone. Skill frontmatter includes preamble-tier (1=heavy preamble, 3=light) — useful for corpus where /init-vault needs less preamble than /ingest.

Claude Code plugin shape: .claude-plugin/plugin.json at root. Top-level dirs: commands/ (flat .md slash commands), skills/<name>/SKILL.md (progressive-disclosure workflows with references/, scripts/, assets/ subdirs), agents/<name>.md (subagents), hooks/hooks.json, bin/ (added to PATH), .mcp.json, .lsp.json, monitors/monitors.json, settings.json, themes/. Variable ${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT} expands to plugin root in shell injections (```bash blocks and !` ` markers). Verified spelling. Source: anthropic-skills:skill-creator + claude-code-guide research 2026-04-28.

Plugin manifest fields: name (required, lowercase-hyphens, becomes namespace prefix /plugin-name:skill); version (semver, falls back to git SHA); description, author, homepage, repository, license, keywords; dependencies (array of strings or {name, version} objects with semver ranges, auto-installed); userConfig (object map: each key has {description, sensitive}; user prompted at install time, values stored in plugin local settings, encrypted if sensitive; runtime access pattern is [unverified] — likely env var or JSON file in plugin dir); settings (defaults applied on enable). Distribution: claude plugin install <git-url> or marketplace. Marketplace submission via claude.ai/settings/plugins/submit.

userConfig values exposed two ways at runtime: (1) substitution as ${user_config.KEY} in MCP/LSP configs, hook commands, monitor commands, and (for non-sensitive) skill/agent content; (2) exported to plugin subprocesses as CLAUDE_PLUGIN_OPTION_<KEY> env vars. Non-sensitive also stored in settings.json under pluginConfigs[<plugin-id>].options. NO auto $CORPUS_VAULT env var — if we want vaultPath userConfig as $CORPUS_VAULT, must use SessionStart hook to export it. Source: code.claude.com/docs/en/plugins-reference.md#user-configuration (verified 2026-04-28).
